entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGY CROPS
entities.description: Crops developed using biotechnology for improved traits.

entities.name: GENETIC MODIFICATION
entities.description: The process of altering the genetic material of an organism to introduce desired traits.

entities.name: BACILLUS THURINGIENSIS
entities.description: A soil-dwelling bacterium used as a source of insecticidal proteins.

entities.name: BT ENDOTOXINS
entities.description: Toxins produced by Bacillus thuringiensis that provide insect resistance to crops.

entities.name: INSECT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand or repel insect pests.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC CROPS
entities.description: Crops that have been genetically engineered to contain genes from other species.

entities.name: GENE STACKING
entities.description: The strategy of incorporating multiple genes into a single crop to enhance resistance.

entities.name: MODIFIED BT TOXINS
entities.description: Bt toxins that have been altered to improve efficacy or overcome pest resistance.

entities.name: VEGETATIVE INSECTICIDAL PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins produced by Bt during its vegetative growth phase used for pest control.

entities.name: LECTINS
entities.description: Carbohydrate-binding proteins used as a mechanism for insect resistance.

entities.name: ENDOGENOUS RESISTANCE
entities.description: Natural resistance mechanisms inherent to the plant species.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions characterized by lower economic development where GM crops are adopted.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers managing small land holdings, often in resource-poor settings.

entities.name: PEST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of insect pests to evolve and survive exposure to biotech crop toxins.
